text:
Once upon a midsummer evening, there was a little girl named Clarice. She loved wandering through the woods behind her house. Tonight was particularly special; the moon was huge and bright, casting eerie shadows everywhere.

Clipper her little kitten loved chasing squirrels. Tonight, however, one peculiar squirrel had ventured too close to town. Clarice thought maybe it needed help. She followed it along beanstalk paths.

Finally, they arrived at a round castle among the clouds. The squirrel looked up nervous.

Clarlice peered inside. There were strange cobwebs and mysterious moonsilver chairs. Suddenly, two mice wearing velvet caps sat listening.

"One fetch my cheese."

"The castle keeps cats."

Clar-lite looked closer. They smiled nicely.

She picked up cheese.

Silks rustled. They were gentlemen!

Three weeks later, she had three feathered friends.

Why did you flee?

Just wandering.

The stars! We've never seen one!

We went to school.

Maybe. Still here.

Would
